ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended, locked) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last successful login */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of consecutive failed login attempts */,
  "external_id" TEXT /* External ID from identity providers (e.g., Google, Microsoft) */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL for the user's profile picture */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user details were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role's responsibilities */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates if the role has administrative privileges */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the role is a default role */,
  "is_locked" INTEGER /* Indicates if the role is locked (e.g., for maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user-role association */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who assigned the role */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_assigned_by FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the session */,
  "token" TEXT /* Unique token for the session */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's session */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's session */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Indicates if the session is the current active session */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., mobile, desktop) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the session was created */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the session expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., read, write) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the permission allows */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the permission is a default permission */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Indicates if the permission is critical (e.g., for security audits) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the role-permission association */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission assigned to the role */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who assigned the permission */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the permission was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_assigned_by FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_permission_id FOREIGN KEY ("permission_id") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the action */,
  "success" INTEGER /* Indicates if the action was successful */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_resets" (
  "reset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password reset request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user requesting the reset */,
  "reset_token" TEXT /* Unique token for the reset request */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the reset request was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the reset request */,
  "is_used" INTEGER /* Indicates if the reset token has been used */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the reset request was created */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the reset token expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_resets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "multi_factor_auth" (
  "mfa_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MFA setting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the MFA setting */,
  "mfa_type" TEXT /* Type of MFA (e.g., SMS, email, TOTP) */,
  "is_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates whether MFA is enabled for the user */,
  "secret_key" TEXT /* Secret key for TOTP MFA */,
  "backup_codes" TEXT /* Backup codes for MFA */,
  "last_used" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last time MFA was used */,
  "recovery_codes_used" INTEGER /* Number of recovery codes used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mfa_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_multi_factor_auth_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "failed_logins" (
  "failed_login_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each failed login attempt */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the failed attempt */,
  "attempted_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the failed attempt occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the attempt was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the failed attempt */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the failed attempt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("failed_login_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_failed_logins_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tokens" (
  "token_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API token */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the API token */,
  "token" TEXT /* Unique token for API access */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the token was generated */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the token generation */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., mobile, desktop) */,
  "is_revoked" INTEGER /* Indicates if the token has been revoked */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the token was created */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the token expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("token_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tokens_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
